items.title: Vertical Infinity
items.summary: If you said to me. Al, what time frame defines you?…
items.content_html:
If you said to me.

Al, what time frame defines you?

I'd say back:

Don't call me Al.

Only my friends do.

And I'd go with the 80s.

Growing up in NYC in the 1980s was something special.

Doing it with two constantly working parents made it downright unreal.

Other than school, I was free.

Free range.

My family ran a fruit and vegetables market in Manhattan.

It was really a grocery store, but that's what I called it back then.

I was too young to do much of anything besides get in the way.

But you'd occasionally find me behind the cash register, ringing up people a little too quickly for their liking.

I was just trying to show off.

For the most part, I was free to do anything I wanted.

Within reason.

Don't get in trouble.

Be safe.

And by the grace of God, I didn't.

And I was.

One time, a normally dressed man saw me video gaming in a bodega.

He asked me if I was ok.

Which confused me.

Yes, why?

He handed me his card.

NYPD.

If you need help, call me.

I found the whole thing so strange.

Only in looking back, I see the angels.

It wasn't only the boys in blue either.

Some of the infamous folk around town looked out for me, too.

"Leave him alone. He cool."

And so everyone did.

Let me game.

Let me wander.

In peace.

On my adventure.

They protected me in a "Don't turn out like us, little man" way.

Shielding me from reality.

So I could continue living my dream.

As crowned prince of corner video games.

I don't know if that happens anymore.

Moved away from all of its city lights decades ago.

But I hope.

I hope.

No matter what side we're on.

We agree to protect and look out for kids.

To let them be kids for as long as possible.

Within reason.

I didn't leave my heart in the Big Apple.

I took it with me.

New Amsterdam.

And my heart.

But I repeat myself.

As I'm wont to do.

items.title: Exceptions and Rules
items.summary: If you had to pick. Do you pick syllables or letter…
items.content_html:
If you had to pick.

Do you pick syllables or letter count?

What the?

I'm serious.

In a word.

Or a name.

What's more important?

Less syllables or less letters?

I like a many syllable many letter word now and again.

But I am growing to appreciate two syllable words.

Or even phrases.

Two syllable phrases.

Bang bang!

I was an English major, but I couldn't really tell you what an adjective is.

I just know.

I will say, one syllable is too few.

Right?

No.

One letter, also.

Gotta have more.

But not too much.

One of my favorite words has six syllables!

Thirteen letters.

A big word. 

Bigger meaning.

I couldn't tell you what an adjective is.

But I could talk for hours about syllables and letter counts.

Two syllables. Maybe three.

Character count don't really matter.

In this case.

Only this case.

Of course, some of the most important words have one syllable...

Oh, God...

Right. 

Like God.

items.title: You Or Something Like You
items.summary: How soon before we no longer interact with a person, but…
items.content_html:
How soon before we no longer interact with a person, but their ai agent that pretends to be the person?

Emails. Phone calls. Video conferences. Texts.

I'm not even talking about scams.

I'm talking legit people.

Sending out agents to be them.

Meetings seem the obvious choice.

Send your agents to take notes and summarize.

Pointing out relevant sheet.

This could have been an email meeting can now be an email.

Is that happening now?

And can you back out of a deal or something if your agent agrees but you ultimately do not?

Is it binding?

Can you be bound by the actions of your authorized ai agent?

I would think so.

Even, especially, if it goes rogue.

Who are you hiring, the person or the agent?

Will it even matter at a certain point?

I would hope you know I write every dang word here.

No surprise there.

I own it.

Rightly or otherwise.

No machine makes this sheet up.

I'd want my money back if it did.

items.title: Did You Eat?
items.summary: Hello there. Welcome back to this wonderful musings column. Where I…
items.content_html:
Hello there.

Welcome back to this wonderful musings column.

Where I write about whatever I want.

Within reason.

That's what I tell my kids when we go out to eat.

You can have anything you want.

Within reason.

If I were unreasonable, which happens from time to time, this place would be unfit for human consumption.

Maybe ai could eat, but you? 

No.

Unless you're ai.

Then you could eat.

Life is this crazy dance between being reasonable and unreasonable.

Too much reason and you're meh.

Never be meh.

Too much unreason and you're likely shackled.

Never stay caged. Unless you're in a steel cage match, but I digress. Well, actually especially in a steel cage match, because the whole point of it is to win and you can do that by leaving it. But I digress twice.

The joy is in the middle.

Sweet spot.

Goldilocks.

Just right.

Be unreasonable.

Within reason.

items.title: Sniff Test - Pass / Fail (Circle One)
items.summary: What's that called when you attract and repel certain things? Magnetism.
items.content_html:
What's that called when you attract and repel certain things?

Magnetism.

Like typos in scam emails.

Supposedly done intentionally to filter out those who would easily realize it's a scam so that only the remaining susceptible-to-typos folk are left.

I'm not convinced it's intentional, but whatevs.

I don't care.

It works on a lot of levels, too.

Take accolades.

One brags and points to an accomplishment that attracts some people and repels those who know a bit more of what's involved in actually gaining it.

Like those books back in the day that were directories featuring only the best of the best.

Only to find out you just had to buy a listing.

Looks impressive to those who don't know.

"As featured in...."

💨

Creates a shudder in those that do.

Like saying you're a best selling author to the world but really it was in a niche ebook category. 

Or a "true" best selling author but you just bought all of the books yourself.

Not a bad idea.

I ain't trying to hate vehemently dislike.

I'm just telling it like it is. 

Or, rather, as I see it.

Which is how it is.

There are too many people out there.

Gotta narrow it down somehow.

Who you repulse, I'd argue, is even more important than who you attract.

Numbers wise.

Be repulsive to those you don't want.

Attract those that remain.

Everything is a signal.

Even the noise.

Proof's in the pudding.

Whatever that means.*

🧲

Push pull. 

 

* The phrase comes from the 17th-century proverb, "The proof of the pudding is in the eating." Originally, "proof" meant "test". Medieval puddings were savory meat-and-grain sausages. Since meat preservation was unreliable, the only way to test if a pudding was safe and well-made was to eat it.

items.title: Nappeun Nom
items.summary: When I lived in the Bay, I used to go to…
items.content_html:
When I lived in the Bay, I used to go to this wonderful little Korean restaurant by myself.

I'd eat Korean BBQ and kimchi stew.

It was owned, as most Korean restaurants should be, by a family.

The cook should be the head of said Korean family, man or woman, the older the better, ideally grand (or a Mexican man - is that racist? I don't mean it to be), but I digress.

And the mother used to come by and chit chat.

Small talk.

Do a little recon.

As Korean mothers do.

And, one day, after I'd become a regular, she asked me if she could set me up.

With a nice Korean girl.

She knew and approved of many.

And I told her, in my broken Korean, no thank you.

And when she asked why, I made up something.

I said, in clear Korean, "Because I'm a bad man."

Looking back, I see there's more truth to that statement that I'd like to have admitted back then.

But I simply didn't want to "ruin" my sanctuary.

My circle of comfort food.

She looked rightfully confused and shocked.

But never mentioned it again.

Even grew a little distant.

It's the way she should have kept it.

I suppose I stopped going there soon after anyways.

I don't remember a single thing I loved there.

Spicy pork, maybe?

But it was good.

Good food.

Healed me a bit.

Vibe was ruined after that.

I was a vibe guy.

And what can I say, some things never change.

"Hard work pays off."

"Dreams come true."

"Bad times don't last,"

"But bad guys do."

items.title: Earth, Wind & Fire
items.summary: Emotion versus logic. These two sometimes fight. You get too emotional,…
items.content_html:
Emotion versus logic.

These two sometimes fight.

You get too emotional, no logic makes sense.

Can't reason with extreme emotions.

Like talking to water or fire.

And you can't emotionally appeal to the heart of logic.

It either is or isn't.

There are no extenuating (spelled that one without the cursed red line appearing - first try - boom) circumstances.

No exceptions.

How much we depend on either when making a choice in life really will tell us a lot about how we live it.

Are we mostly emotionally driven?

Or are we logically?

Sometimes, following your heart will lead to your downfall.

Sometimes, following your logic will lead to a boring existence.

But it works the other way, too.

Follow your heart to find true love.

Follow your logic to make wise decisions.

Like most things, I'm somewhere on the spectrum.

After all, following your heart makes for the best stories.

Following your logic, however, makes you able to live to muse about them.

Fire, water, and the ground beneath your feet.

💨

Excuse me, shouldn't have had broccoli.

items.title: Always Pick C
items.summary: What is life about? What's it all mean? Is it about…
items.content_html:
What is life about?

What's it all mean?

Is it about reaching your potential?

Or keeping the world from taking it away?

What I mean is, are you born maxed out and the world slowly beats you down or do you start at Level 1 and (hopefully) go up from there?

There's a bit of truth in both.

Like many of the best choices, it's a little of both.

A or B choice.

Pick C.

"What was it we had for dinner tonight?"

"Well, we had a choice of steak or fish."

"Yes, yes, I remember. I had lasagna."

✈️

There's a duality at play here.

We all come out the gate perfect.

And yet we gotta level up.

World beats us down.

Still we rise.

Perfect noob.

Beaten up warrior monk.

With the scars and memories to prove it.

When I was a child, my heroes were perfect.

Shimmering.

Flawless.

Unbeatable.

As I grew older, I learned some things.

Beaten down.

Leveled up.

And my definition of hero changed.

They wore well-worn, not fancy, items.

Their faces and hands were their resumes.

They were defeated, at one time or another, but they did not break.

Permanently.

I believe in Kintsugi.

Golden joinery.

Golden scars.

Beauty in the broken.

Rebuilt better than ever.

Stronger than ever.

So I ask, where are you on the scale of things?

Still sprinting from the womb?

Or ready to hang your sword above the fireplace?

Or somewhere in between.

To heroes.

Particularly the broken.

And to comebacks.

Get up, Champ.

items.title: The Right To Remain Vocal
items.summary: You might be asking yourself: "Self, why does this old(ish) man…
items.content_html:
You might be asking yourself:

"Self, why does this old(ish) man keep coming back here every day to write a musings column?"

Are you asking yourself that?

And if so, I got an answer for you.

In the form of a question:

Why not?

I do that a lot, you know.

I ask a question when asked a question.

Another tip.

You are not obligated to answer questions.

People think so.

I'm just being polite.

Meh.

Polite can be cruel.

Both ways.

Also, you can also pretend no speaky Engrish.

But you might have to have the right avatar for that.

And so I'm using my right.

To muse.

Amuse myself.

Maybe one or two others out there.

Thank you, check's in the mail.

I write because there are universes where I cannot.

I write because I should not be here.

I write because I am still am.

And what takes a lickin'.

Keeps on musin'.

See ya.

Wouldn't want to be ya.

[The author sings in a cappella (this is two words?!?) for an uncomfortably long time in an amateurish tone at best but does so with staggering confidence. A song he makes up the lyrics to as he goes. He pauses at times searching for words in his brain. Increasing his volume every time he resumes. "AND IIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIEEEEEEIIIIIIIIII!"]

items.title: Worth The Squeeze
items.summary: I almost forgot to write today. Let's change that. What a…
items.content_html:
I almost forgot to write today.

Let's change that.

What a strange time to be alive.

Like all the other times.

But this time is strange, no?

Seems like we're on the verge of something, but it's already happening.

We're in the middle of it.

It's here.

You feel me?

Looking back in a hundred years, folks might be pointing to right now as a pivotal season.

Where it all began to change.

For the better (or something else).

I'm sort of convinced that the more I worry about something the less chance it happens.

If you want peace, prepare for war.

And yet, as anyone will tell you, I am a believer in manifesting your destiny.

What a wonderful wor(l)d.

Every day, in every way, I'm getting better and better.

To peak.

It's a duality that I find confusing, but it's seems yin and yangish, so it's probably fitting.

I'm at peace with who I am.

Even if it's war.

What does this make possible?

Have you lived a life that if you shared it, no one would believe you?

I have.

And I do.

Good and bad.

Bad and good.

The best stories I hold forever in my heart.

Not for public consumption.

Why brag, when no one would believe you, anyways?

And why do they even have to know?

I wish you the very best in this life and beyond.

And that you too have stories too good to share.

I'll believe you.

Just don't tell me.

One for the ages.

What a life.
